steps.title: Convert slope into a second point
steps.content: The point $(2,47)$ is on the graph. Since the slope is $5$, increasing $x$ by $1$ increases $g(x)$ by $5$, so another point is $(3,52)$. Put $(2,47)$ and $(3,52)$ into a Desmos table.

steps.title: Fit the equation
steps.content: Run $y_1 \sim mx_1+b$. Desmos gives $m=5$ and $b=37$, which means the equation is $y=5x+37$.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Choice A has slope $37$, and choice B swaps the slope and intercept. Choice C is not the right expression in terms of $x$. Choice D, $g(x)=5x+37$, has slope $5$ and gives $g(2)=47$. Choice D is correct.
